Here is a complete summary of every TODO found in the `webapp` project:

---

## 📋 TODO Work Items — `projects/webapp`

### 1. `docs/README.md` — Line 30 (`## TODO` section)
Three planned features are listed in the project's README:

| # | Work Item |
|---|-----------|
| 1a | **Add database persistence** — the user store is currently in-memory only |
| 1b | **Add authentication middleware** — no auth layer exists yet |
| 1c | **Add input validation on POST** — POST requests are not validated |

---

### 2. `src/UsersController.scala` — Line 20 (inline code comment)
```scala
case "POST" =>
    // TODO: parse body, validate, add user
```
The `POST /users` handler is **not yet implemented**. It needs to:
- Parse the incoming request body (likely JSON)
- Validate the parsed input
- Insert the new user into the in-memory store (and eventually a real DB per item 1a above)

---

### Summary

| Location | Line | Work Item |
|---|---|---|
| `docs/README.md` | 30 | Add database persistence (currently in-memory) |
| `docs/README.md` | 30 | Add authentication middleware |
| `docs/README.md` | 30 | Add input validation on POST |
| `src/UsersController.scala` | 20 | Parse body, validate, and add user on `POST /users` |

**4 total work items across 2 files.** The `UsersController.scala` TODO directly corresponds to the "input validation on POST" item in the README, so these are tightly related and should likely be tackled together.
